syntax_intro.label: Syntax: Rules Computers Require
syntax_intro.summary: Learn what syntax means and why breaking syntax causes errors like SyntaxError.

intro.title: Syntax: The Rules of a Programming Language
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Every programming language has a set of rules that determine **how instructions must be written**.
These rules are called **syntax**.

Just like spoken languages have rules for how words and sentences must be structured, programming languages have **syntax rules** that define how code must be written so the computer can understand it.

Each programming language has its own syntax:

- Python has its own syntax
- Java has its own syntax
- C has its own syntax

Even though many languages share similar ideas, the **exact way you write instructions** can be different in each language.

If you break these syntax rules, Python will raise a **SyntaxError**, and the program will stop running because the **interpreter cannot understand the instruction**.

In this module, you will begin getting comfortable with Python syntax so you can write **clear and correct instructions** for the computer to execute.

m0_syntax_definition.title: Syntax meaning

options.a: The rules for how code must be written
options.b: The speed of your computer fan

m0_syntax_definition.hint: Syntax = writing rules the language requires.

m0_syntax_syntaxerror.title: SyntaxError
m0_syntax_syntaxerror.prompt: If you break Python’s syntax rules, you often get:
m0_syntax_syntaxerror.hint: SyntaxError means Python couldn’t understand your code structure.

m0_syntax_indentation_rule.title: Indentation matters
m0_syntax_indentation_rule.prompt: Why is indentation important in Python?

options.a: It makes the code run faster
options.b: Python uses it as part of its syntax (structure)

m0_syntax_indentation_rule.hint: Indentation is part of Python’s structure, not just style.

m0_syntax_fill_rules_word.title: Syntax means rules
m0_syntax_fill_rules_word.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
m0_syntax_fill_rules_word.template: Syntax is the set of ____ for writing code.
m0_syntax_fill_rules_word.hint: Syntax tells you how code must be written.

m0_syntax_fill_error_name.title: Error name
m0_syntax_fill_error_name.prompt: Complete the sentence with the correct term.
m0_syntax_fill_error_name.template: Breaking Python syntax often causes a ____.
m0_syntax_fill_error_name.hint: It is the common Python error for bad structure.

m0_syntax_fill_python_structure.title: Important Python structure
m0_syntax_fill_python_structure.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
m0_syntax_fill_python_structure.template: In Python, ____ is part of the language structure.
m0_syntax_fill_python_structure.hint: Spaces at the start of a line can matter.

m0_syntax_fill_understand_code.title: Why syntax matters
m0_syntax_fill_understand_code.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
m0_syntax_fill_understand_code.template: Syntax rules help the interpreter ____ your code.

m0_syntax_fill_language_name.title: Language example
m0_syntax_fill_language_name.prompt: Complete the sentence with the correct language name.

m0_syntax_reorder_write_flow.title: Order the writing flow
m0_syntax_reorder_write_flow.prompt: Reorder the steps for writing valid code.

m0_syntax_reorder_breaking_rules.title: Order the error flow
m0_syntax_reorder_breaking_rules.prompt: Reorder what happens when syntax is broken.
m0_syntax_reorder_breaking_rules.hint: The error happens after the rule is broken.

m0_syntax_reorder_indent_idea.title: Order the indentation idea
m0_syntax_reorder_indent_idea.prompt: Reorder the parts into the main idea.

m0_syntax_reorder_language_rules.title: Order the language rule idea
m0_syntax_reorder_language_rules.prompt: Reorder the words into a correct idea.

m0_syntax_reorder_clear_code_goal.title: Order the coding goal
m0_syntax_reorder_clear_code_goal.prompt: Reorder the parts into a sensible goal.
